title: carex subscabrella
intro: Carex subscabrella, also known as the short-spiked sedge, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Carex genus and is native to eastern North America. The medicinal properties of Carex subscabrella lie in its leaves, which have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues. The plant is believed to have diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties, and its health benefits include the treatment of urinary tract infections, fever reduction, and relief from respiratory problems. In herbal preparations, Carex subscabrella can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health conditions.
